meta.title: Rich text input
meta.description: The rich text input component allows users to input and format text using a user friendly interface. Changing standard text inputs into fully featured edits, supporting formatting, linking, and embedding.

toc.value: Working with Slate.js and HTML values

guidelines.mdx: ## Guidelines These guidelines provide comprehensive direction for designing and developing rich text inputs within Nimbus. ### Best practices - **Make responsive changes:** Apply changes immediately to the selected text upon clicking a toolbar control. - **Utilize tooltips and labels:** Include tooltips for all toolbar actions to enhance discoverability and accessibility. - **Keep a user's focus:** Maintain the user's focus within the text area after an action or toggle is selected. - **Allow for common shortcuts:** Support standard keyboard shortcuts for formatting (e.g., Ctrl/Cmd+B for bold). - **Make it easier to correct mistakes:** Offer an undo/redo history for content changes, and allow for a large enough input window so a user can quickly read content without needing to scroll line by line. - **Tailor tooling:** Determine the required formatting options based on the user's needs for the specific application. - **Curate tools into meaningful similar collections:** Group related controls logically in the toolbar (e.g., text styling, lists, media). ## Usage Rich text inputs should be used in areas where a user knows that a longer response or description is expected. > [!TIP]\ > When to use - **Editing long-form content:** Such as product descriptions, knowledge base articles, or email bodies, where visual hierarchy and style are necessary for readability. - **Formatting text:** Such as applying bold, italics, underlines, or specific heading levels. - **Structuring content:** With bulleted or numbered lists. - **Embedding external elements:** Like links, images, or videos within other content. > [!CAUTION]\ > When not to use - **Short answers:** Or single-line inputs, such as names, titles, or search queries. - **Specific data entry:** Unformatted text is required, like SKUs, passwords, or simple codes. - **Plain text communication:** Such as chat messages or comments, where formatting options can clutter the interface or distract from the conversation. - **Inputs that need to be strictly controlled:** Where the application's style guide should be the only source of formatting (e.g., when there is passing through of styling and content to another site). ### Rich text input in use > [!TIP]\ > **Do** > > - Do use tooltips for tools represented by icons. In the case of paragraph > styles show the style in the dropdown, not just stated in plain text. > - Do provide more height for the input to see a few lines of text and reduce > scrolling. > - Do utilize an undo and redo button to assist a user with writing and editing > text when possible. ```jsx live const App = () => ( ); ``` > [!CAUTION]\ > **Don't** > > - Don't make the input so small that you cannot easily see a few lines of > text. > - Don't move the rich text toolbar to sit on the bottom of the input. > - Don't overload the rich text toolbar with tools that are unnecessary. ```jsx live const App = () => ( ); ```
